---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-SLIDES
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-SLIDES
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-SLIDES
id: ESE111-CH04-SLIDES-en
resource_type: slides
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-SLIDES-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
format: revealjs
outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract:
  outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: pending-restricted-store
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# One suspicious reading can test an entire network

At TP-B, a technician expects about 1.13 V but reads nearly 0 V. Replace R4,
rebuild the model, or test something else first?

::: notes
0–4 min. Silent individual choice with reason and confidence; accept paper, raised
card, keyboard poll, or partner-mediated response. Do not reveal a fault. Ask which
possible result would reverse the choice. The local lamp context is not approved;
describe this as a generic repair fixture.
:::

## Voltage needs two nodes; current needs a chosen direction

Draw COM, one voltage polarity, and one current arrow. What would a negative result mean?

::: notes
4–9 min. Retrieval. Expected: voltage difference relative to COM; negative means
opposite to the declared arrow/polarity. Surface “negative equals wrong” and
“COM equals protective earth.” Keep original prediction visible.
:::

## Prediction is evidence before it is an answer

![Controlled two-node network.](../../shared/figures/ese111-ch04-network.svg){fig-alt="A 6 V source feeds R1 to TP-A; R2 connects TP-A to COM; R3 connects TP-A to TP-B; R4 connects TP-B to COM."}

Rank without calculation: $V_A$, $V_B$, 0 V. Is $I_1=I_2$?

::: notes
9–15 min. Poll first, pair explanation second. Expected $V_A>V_B>0$, and
$I_1=I_2+I_3$. Record the current-consumed misconception. Static SVG and its
text reading replace animation.
:::

## A node equation is a physical inventory

At TP-A:

\[
\frac{V_A-6}{10\,000}+\frac{V_A}{22\,000}+
\frac{V_A-V_B}{33\,000}=0
\]

::: notes
15–25 min. Build each term on the board only after a learner names the branch and
direction. Ask what branch would be missing if the equation had two terms. French
caution: use *courant électrique*, *nœud*, *branche*; symbols do not change.
:::

## The second node closes the model

\[
\frac{V_B-V_A}{33\,000}+\frac{V_B}{15\,000}=0
\]

Therefore $V_A=3.2V_B$.

::: notes
25–31 min. Learners complete the rearrangement individually. A wrong factor of
2.2 often reveals inversion of 33/15. Ask for a limiting check when R4 becomes very small.
:::

## An estimate can reject a polished wrong answer

Before solving:

- $I_1<6/10\text{ kΩ}=0.6\text{ mA}$
- $0<V_B<V_A<6\text{ V}$
- $V_B$ is roughly one third of $V_A$

::: notes
31–36 min. Ask learners to test two offered calculator results, one with $V_B>V_A$.
The estimate is a decision gate, not decorative arithmetic.
:::

## Three independent checks make the solution defensible

| Result | Nominal |
|---|---:|
| $V_A$ | 3.608 V |
| $V_B$ | 1.128 V |
| $I_1$ | 239.18 µA |
| $I_2$ | 164.01 µA |
| $I_3=I_4$ | 75.17 µA |

Check both nodes, two loops, and power.

::: notes
36–45 min. Fade the worked calculation: learners supply one missing current and
one KCL equality. Power is an independent sanity check. Retain units throughout.
:::

## The model is useful because its limits are explicit

Steady DC, lumped nodes, ohmic resistors, stable temperature, regulated source,
and represented instruments.

::: notes
45–50 min. Ask “Which assumption fails first at high frequency?” and “Does an
open branch necessarily have zero voltage?” Keep the field-model limitation short;
do not expand into later-course electromagnetics.
:::

## A voltmeter is another branch, not a spectator

Unloaded TP-B: 1.1276 V  
With 1 MΩ at TP-B: 1.1154 V and $I_M=1.115\ \mu\text{A}$

::: notes
50–60 min. Run the physical demo only after approval; otherwise show the static
calculated pair and prepared-data row. Prediction poll: higher/lower/same. Add
$I_M$ to KCL-B. Never generalize 1 or 10 MΩ to an unverified meter.
:::

## A residual is a question, not a mark to erase

\[
r=\text{measured}-\text{predicted},\qquad U=k u_c,\qquad |r|\le U\ ?
\]

What should be checked when the residual exceeds its declared bound?

::: notes
60–67 min. Learners rank source, actual R, meter loading, reference, contact, and
topology. Require the recorded coverage factor $k$ whenever expanded uncertainty
$U$ is used. This compatibility check is not a universal hypothesis test. Negative
data remains in the record.
:::

## The best next test separates hypotheses before replacement

Symptom: $V_B\approx0$, $V_A$ plausible.

- H1: R3 open
- H2: R4 short/too low
- H3: wrong COM

Choose one safe test and predict all three outcomes.

::: notes
67–76 min. Require outcome predictions before vote. Preferred sequence begins with
source/reference confirmation and a de-energized discriminating branch test. Do
not reveal the controlled pilot variant.
:::

## Evidence changes R4, then verification changes the claim

New requirements: $1.40\le V_B\le1.52\text{ V}$, $I_S\le0.25\text{ mA}$.

Choose 18, 22, 27, or 33 kΩ. Nominal success is not enough.

::: notes
76–84 min. Groups choose, state margin and loading assumption, then compare with
the simulated 22 kΩ corner range 1.412–1.500 V. Emphasize that only the authorized
post-change physical test verifies hardware.
:::

## Exit evidence chooses the next learning route

A 1 MΩ meter reads TP-B=1.115 V versus 1.128 V unloaded. Name:

1. the first model revision;
2. one disconfirming measurement;
3. one claim you still cannot make.

::: notes
84–90 min. Classify individually: correct new branch + discriminating check → lab;
branch named but no equation → REM-loading; “bad law/meter” without test → REM-debug;
unsafe mode → REM-dmm before lab. Collect responses. Static PDF/HTML, the board
sequence, and prepared table are the no-power/no-internet fallback.
:::
